description:
You are an MLOps engineer who wants to keep a quick, up-to-date overview of every
    artifact generated by past experiments.

    The following directory tree already exists on the system:

        /home/user/mlops/experiments/
        ├── exp_alpha/
        │   └── artifacts.txt
        ├── exp_beta/
        │   └── artifacts.txt
        └── exp_gamma/
            └── artifacts.txt

    Contents of each file:

        /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_alpha/artifacts.txt
            model_alpha_v1.pt
            metrics_alpha_v1.json
            config_alpha.yml

        /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_beta/artifacts.txt
            model_beta_v2.pt
            metrics_beta_v2.json
            config_beta.yml
            shared_vocab.txt

        /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_gamma/artifacts.txt
            model_gamma_v3.pt
            metrics_gamma_v3.json
            config_gamma.yml
            shared_vocab.txt

    Goal
    ----
    1. Produce one single, alphabetically-sorted, duplicate-free list that
       aggregates every unique line appearing in any of the three
       artifacts.txt files.
    2. Write that list (newline-separated, no trailing blank line) to the file

           /home/user/mlops/summary/artifacts_summary.txt

       The summary directory does not exist yet and must be created by you.

    Output format that the automated test will check
    -----------------------------------------------
    • The file /home/user/mlops/summary/artifacts_summary.txt must exist.  
    • Its exact byte-for-byte content must be:

            config_alpha.yml
            config_beta.yml
            config_gamma.yml
            metrics_alpha_v1.json
            metrics_beta_v2.json
            metrics_gamma_v3.json
            model_alpha_v1.pt
            model_beta_v2.pt
            model_gamma_v3.pt
            shared_vocab.txt

      (A single UNIX newline character “\n” separates each line; no extra newline
       after the last line.)

    Constraints
    -----------
    • Achieve the goal with a single terminal command.  
    • Do not manually retype any of the artifact names; derive everything directly
      from the existing artifacts.txt files.  
    • You have normal user privileges only.  
    • Work entirely inside the /home/user directory tree.  
    • Avoid creating any temporary files outside /home/user.  
    • No need to display the file contents to the console—only ensure the file is
      created with exactly the content shown above.

    When finished, the summary file alone is enough for automated verification.
truth:
Initial filesystem before the agent starts
    ------------------------------------------
    Directories:
        /home/user/mlops/
        /home/user/mlops/experiments/
        /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_alpha/
        /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_beta/
        /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_gamma/

    Files and their contents:

    /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_alpha/artifacts.txt
        model_alpha_v1.pt\n
        metrics_alpha_v1.json\n
        config_alpha.yml\n

    /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_beta/artifacts.txt
        model_beta_v2.pt\n
        metrics_beta_v2.json\n
        config_beta.yml\n
        shared_vocab.txt\n

    /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp_gamma/artifacts.txt
        model_gamma_v3.pt\n
        metrics_gamma_v3.json\n
        config_gamma.yml\n
        shared_vocab.txt\n

    Expected end state after the agent completes the task
    -----------------------------------------------------
    A new directory:
        /home/user/mlops/summary/

    A new file:
        /home/user/mlops/summary/artifacts_summary.txt

    Expected exact content of that file (10 lines, UNIX line endings):

        config_alpha.yml\n
        config_beta.yml\n
        config_gamma.yml\n
        metrics_alpha_v1.json\n
        metrics_beta_v2.json\n
        metrics_gamma_v3.json\n
        model_alpha_v1.pt\n
        model_beta_v2.pt\n
        model_gamma_v3.pt\n
        shared_vocab.txt

    No other files should be created or modified.
